title: Get started with lssdoc
text: read_lss() reads .lss (XML) export structured object, preserving every user-entered string verbatim.
code: lss <- read_lss(demo) lss$languages #> [1] "en" "de" "es" "fr"

title: Get started with lssdoc
text: audit_lss() flags reviewable anomalies – missing translations, duplicate codes, forward filter references, array-scale inconsistencies, orphan references – classed object print() method. demo survey clean; deliberately broken fixture included show output:
code: broken <- system.file("extdata", "audit_demo.lss", package = "lssdoc") audit_lss(read_lss(broken)) #>  #> ── lssdoc audit ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> File: /tmp/Rtmpfnd2Pc/temp_libpath1a22310f446e/lssdoc/extdata/audit_demo.lss #> Languages: "en" and "fr" #> 12 findings: 5 errors, 7 warnings, 0 notes. #> ✖ Survey: Duplicate question code: 'age'. #> ✖ Question 'blank_q': The question text is empty in every language. #> ✖ Question 'age': Filter references variable 'income' (item 4), which is not #>   asked before this question (item 1). #> ✖ Answer 'X': Answer points to question id '99999', which does not exist. #> ✖ Subquestion 'orphan_sq': Subquestion points to question id '99999', which #>   does not exist. #> Question 'arr': Subquestions reference scale_id '0' but no answer options are #> defined for it. #> Question 'arr': Answer options reference scale_id '1' but no subquestions are #> defined for it. #> Question 'comment ': The question code 'comment ' contains whitespace; #> LimeSurvey will export it verbatim, which usually breaks downstream lookups. #> Group: The group name is empty in every language. #> Question 'satisf': Type 'Single choice' expects answer options, but none are #> defined. #> Question 'rating': Type 'Multiple choice' expects subquestions, but none are #> defined. #> Question 'income' [fr]: The question text is missing in 'fr' but present in #> other languages.

title: Get started with lssdoc
text: render_questionnaire() writes document; output format inferred file extension (.docx .pdf). Rendering uses suggested packages . Two templates available. "cards" template (default) stacks one block per item – closest printed questionnaire:  "table" template one dense table covering whole document – one row per variable, value codes beneath:
code: # Parse once (above), then render different variants without re-reading. render_questionnaire(lss, "review.docx") render_questionnaire(lss, "questionnaire.docx", template = "table")

title: Get started with lssdoc
text: languages – content columns appear, order (c("en", "fr")); first primary language. chrome_lang – language document chrome (column headers, row labels, type labels), independent content languages. template – "cards" (default) "table". variable_names – "brackets" (default) reproduces LimeSurvey CSV/Excel export column names, variable index matches raw data file; "underscore" uses SPSS/Stata code form. base_size – body type size points (default 10); raise (e.g. 12) roomier single-language render. page_format – "auto", "A4-portrait", "A4-landscape" "A3".
code: render_questionnaire(   lss, "review_en_fr.docx",   languages   = c("en", "fr"),   template    = "table",   chrome_lang = "en" )

title: Get started with lssdoc
text: Pass .pdf path: package writes .docx temporary location converts locally via LibreOffice (must installed PATH). upload, network call.
code: render_questionnaire(lss, "review.pdf")

title: Get started with lssdoc
text: render_audit() produces focused QA document – cover page, one section per severity – separate full review.
code: render_audit(lss, "qa.docx")

what: lssdoc: Word and PDF questionnaire documents from LimeSurvey '.lss' files
title: lssdoc: Word and PDF questionnaire documents from LimeSurvey '.lss' files
text: lssdoc turns LimeSurvey .lss export polished Word (.docx) PDF questionnaire document anyone working LimeSurvey survey: researchers, survey methodologists, ethics committees, translators reviewers. renders questionnaire content side side four languages, runs automated integrity audit, produces layout reads published instrument – developer dump. Two output templates: "cards" – one detached block per item (meta table + question table), stacked vertically. Closest printed questionnaire respondent see.  "table" – one dense table covering whole document: every variable one tinted Question row carrying metadata, followed one white Value rows holding answer codes labels per language. Group banners (description, present), welcome text, survey description end text become labelled rows table document reads single artifact.  things lssdoc takes care : Multilingual chrome. Column headers, row labels, type labels (Single choice / Multiple choice / Text / Number …), Value descriptors, audit section – everything outside survey content – translated English, French, German, Spanish Italian. Independent survey’s content languages, can keep FR/DE questionnaire switch document scaffolding English (vice versa) single argument. Variable-centric compound rendering, matching data file. Every response variable documented exact column name LimeSurvey CSV / Excel export, variable index lines raw data file column column: arrays multiple choice parent[subq] / parent[], dual-scale arrays split one block per scale (parent[subq][1]), two-dimensional arrays row x column cross-product (parent[row_col]), ranking expanded one column per position (parent[answerid]), list--comment parent[_Ccomment]. Pass variable_names = "underscore" Expression Manager / SPSS / Stata code form (parent_subq) instead. Methodological type labels. UI distinctions like “List (radio)” vs “List (dropdown)” collapse “Single choice”, response semantics identical actual codes appear Value section. Type cell reads methodologist’s vocabulary, LimeSurvey’s. Automated audit. Missing translations, duplicate codes, forward references routing filters, array-scale inconsistencies, orphan structural references, types missing required options subquestions – flagged inline rendered item , ask , dedicated section. AI; rule-based reviewable. Local-processing. network calls, third-party services. questionnaire content never leaves machine R runs.

title: lssdoc: Word and PDF questionnaire documents from LimeSurvey '.lss' files
text: Install released version CRAN: latest build R-universe (pre-built, compiler needed): development version GitHub, pak: remotes: package depends officer flextable (declared Suggests – parse audit paths work without ).
code: install.packages("lssdoc") install.packages(   "lssdoc",   repos = c("https://amaltawfik.r-universe.dev", "https://cloud.r-project.org") ) # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("amaltawfik/lssdoc") # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("amaltawfik/lssdoc")

title: lssdoc: Word and PDF questionnaire documents from LimeSurvey '.lss' files
text: public API four functions: audit_lss(), render_questionnaire() render_audit() accept input either path .lss file pre-parsed lss object. output format two renderers inferred extension output (.docx .pdf).

title: lssdoc: Word and PDF questionnaire documents from LimeSurvey '.lss' files
text: output uses "cards" template default, chrome_lang following survey’s primary content language. Open review.docx Word, click Yes prompted update fields (page numbers table contents).
code: library(lssdoc)  # Parse the .lss and render the Word document in a single call. render_questionnaire("survey.lss", "review.docx")

title: lssdoc: Word and PDF questionnaire documents from LimeSurvey '.lss' files
text: languages controls content columns appear order; first one primary language used table contents group headings. chrome_lang independent languages: keep chrome_lang = "en" get English column headers row labels even survey content runs French German – pass chrome_lang = "fr" ("de" / "es" / "") want scaffolding follow content.
code: render_questionnaire(   "survey.lss",   "review.docx",   languages   = c("en", "fr"),   template    = "table",   chrome_lang = "en" )

title: lssdoc: Word and PDF questionnaire documents from LimeSurvey '.lss' files
text: cover page picks authors block (clickable ORCID hyperlinks), description (URLs auto-detected made clickable), privacy / admin settings rows metadata table.
code: render_questionnaire(   "survey.lss",   "ethics_review.docx",   languages              = c("en", "fr"),   template               = "table",   chrome_lang            = "en",   show_privacy_settings  = TRUE,   # anonymized / save partial / IP / referrer / timestamp   show_admin_settings    = TRUE,   # alias / end URL / active   authors = list(     list(name        = "Jane Doe",          affiliation = "HESAV",          orcid       = "0009-0001-2345-6789"),     list(name        = "John Doe",          affiliation = "HESAV",          orcid       = "0009-0002-3456-7890")   ),   description = paste0(     "Validated as part of the SNSF project XYZ. ",     "Reference: https://example.org/projects/xyz"   ) )

title: lssdoc: Word and PDF questionnaire documents from LimeSurvey '.lss' files
text: Pure data: one row per variable header, one row per value code, nothing else.
code: render_questionnaire(   "survey.lss",   "minimal.docx",   template         = "table",   show_welcome     = FALSE,   show_endtext     = FALSE,   show_description = FALSE,   show_groups      = FALSE,   show_toc         = FALSE,   show_audit       = FALSE,   show_index       = FALSE )

title: lssdoc: Word and PDF questionnaire documents from LimeSurvey '.lss' files
code: # Parse a single time, then audit and render different variants # without re-reading the .lss. lss   <- read_lss("survey.lss") audit <- audit_lss(lss) print(audit)  # console summary of flagged anomalies  render_questionnaire(lss, "review_en.docx", languages = "en") render_questionnaire(lss, "review_fr.docx", languages = "fr") render_audit(lss, "qa_followup.docx")

title: lssdoc: Word and PDF questionnaire documents from LimeSurvey '.lss' files
text: deliberately flawed survey ships package, can see audit_lss() catches without supplying file:
code: demo <- system.file("extdata", "audit_demo.lss", package = "lssdoc") audit_lss(read_lss(demo)) #> -- lssdoc audit ---------------------------------------------------- #> 12 findings: 5 errors, 7 warnings, 0 notes. #> x Survey: duplicate question code 'age' #> x Question 'age': filter references 'income', asked later (forward reference) #> x Answer 'X': points to a question id that does not exist #> x Subquestion 'orphan_sq': points to a question id that does not exist #> x Question 'blank_q': text empty in every language #> ! Question 'satisf': expects answer options, but none are defined #> ! Question 'income': text missing in 'fr' #> ! Question 'comment ': code carries whitespace #> ... plus an empty group name, missing subquestions and an array-scale mismatch.

title: lssdoc: Word and PDF questionnaire documents from LimeSurvey '.lss' files
code: # Same call, just pass a .pdf path. The package writes the .docx # into a temp file and converts it locally via LibreOffice # (must be installed and on PATH). render_questionnaire("survey.lss", "review.pdf", template = "table") render_audit("survey.lss", "qa.pdf")

title: lssdoc: Word and PDF questionnaire documents from LimeSurvey '.lss' files
text: rendered .docx uses editorial petrol-blue palette tuned publication-grade output (#133B52 primary, #3A7C8C accent, #E9F2F6 #F4F8FA tinted bands, #D3DCE2 soft grid). Calibri body / Consolas monospace default; font font_code arguments accept string override.

what: Audit a LimeSurvey survey for reviewable anomalies — audit_lss
title: Audit a LimeSurvey survey for reviewable anomalies — audit_lss
text: Inspect LimeSurvey survey flag anomalies can detected without AI. audit guides human reviewer; silently correct anything. Every finding names precise location severity.

title: Audit a LimeSurvey survey for reviewable anomalies — audit_lss
code: audit_lss(input)

title: Audit a LimeSurvey survey for reviewable anomalies — audit_lss
text: input Either path .lss file (character string) pre-parsed lss object returned read_lss(). Passing path parses fly; passing lss object avoids re-parsing survey also rendered session.

title: Audit a LimeSurvey survey for reviewable anomalies — audit_lss
text: object class lss_audit: list file, languages, summary counts, findings data frame (severity, check, location, language, message). print() method .data.frame() method.

title: Audit a LimeSurvey survey for reviewable anomalies — audit_lss
text: Checks performed: Missing translations – question, help, answer, subquestion text present least one language empty another. Empty languages – translatable text empty every language. Duplicate codes – question variable code repeated survey, answer/subquestion code repeated within one question. Whitespace codes – question, subquestion answer code containing leading, trailing interior whitespace (likely typo; causes subtle bugs data export). Missing options type – question whose type requires answer options subquestions none (per type taxonomy). Forward filter references – relevance expression names variable appearing filtered question (value yet collected filter evaluated). Array-scale inconsistencies – array (single dual) whose subquestions reference scale_id answer options, vice versa. Orphan references – subquestion answer pointing question exist.

title: Audit a LimeSurvey survey for reviewable anomalies — audit_lss
code: # A deliberately flawed demo survey ships with the package, seeded # with every anomaly the audit detects. demo <- system.file("extdata", "audit_demo.lss", package = "lssdoc") audit_lss(demo) #>  #> ── lssdoc audit ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> File: /tmp/Rtmpfnd2Pc/temp_libpath1a22310f446e/lssdoc/extdata/audit_demo.lss #> Languages: "en" and "fr" #> 12 findings: 5 errors, 7 warnings, 0 notes. #> ✖ Survey: Duplicate question code: 'age'. #> ✖ Question 'blank_q': The question text is empty in every language. #> ✖ Question 'age': Filter references variable 'income' (item 4), which is not #>   asked before this question (item 1). #> ✖ Answer 'X': Answer points to question id '99999', which does not exist. #> ✖ Subquestion 'orphan_sq': Subquestion points to question id '99999', which #>   does not exist. #> Question 'arr': Subquestions reference scale_id '0' but no answer options are #> defined for it. #> Question 'arr': Answer options reference scale_id '1' but no subquestions are #> defined for it. #> Question 'comment ': The question code 'comment ' contains whitespace; #> LimeSurvey will export it verbatim, which usually breaks downstream lookups. #> Group: The group name is empty in every language. #> Question 'satisf': Type 'Single choice' expects answer options, but none are #> defined. #> Question 'rating': Type 'Multiple choice' expects subquestions, but none are #> defined. #> Question 'income' [fr]: The question text is missing in 'fr' but present in #> other languages.

what: lssdoc: Render 'LimeSurvey' '.lss' Questionnaires as Word and PDF Documents — lssdoc-package
title: lssdoc: Render 'LimeSurvey' '.lss' Questionnaires as Word and PDF Documents — lssdoc-package
text: Render 'LimeSurvey' '.lss' survey exports questionnaire documents Word ('.docx') PDF, displaying one four languages side side localized chrome English, French, German, Spanish Italian. Includes rule-based automated audit flags missing translations, forward filter references, duplicate codes, array-scale inconsistencies orphan structural references. Designed anyone working 'LimeSurvey' survey: researchers, methodologists, ethics committees, translators reviewers. Processing fully local: source file input questionnaire content uploaded third-party service.

title: lssdoc: Render 'LimeSurvey' '.lss' Questionnaires as Word and PDF Documents — lssdoc-package
text: Two example .lss files ship package reachable base::system.file(), every reader can reproduce examples Get started vignette without supplying LimeSurvey export: demo_survey.lss – clean, synthetic four-language survey (English, French, German, Spanish) quotas consent block: system.file("extdata", "demo_survey.lss", package = "lssdoc"). audit_demo.lss – deliberately flawed survey seeded every anomaly audit_lss() detects: system.file("extdata", "audit_demo.lss", package = "lssdoc").

title: lssdoc: Render 'LimeSurvey' '.lss' Questionnaires as Word and PDF Documents — lssdoc-package
text: Maintainer: Amal Tawfik amal.tawfik@hesav.ch (ORCID) (ROR) [copyright holder] Authors: Amal Tawfik amal.tawfik@hesav.ch (ORCID) (ROR) [copyright holder]

title: Print an lss_audit object — print.lss_audit
text: Pretty-printed audit summary console, capped first n findings. Severity-based bullet symbols (errors, warnings, notes) mirror shown audit table inside rendered .docx.

title: Print an lss_audit object — print.lss_audit
code: # S3 method for class 'lss_audit' print(x, ..., n = 20L)

title: Print an lss_audit object — print.lss_audit
text: x lss_audit object returned audit_lss(). ... Currently ignored. n Maximum number findings print. Defaults 20. Set Inf print every finding. remaining count, , summarized bottom hint use .data.frame() full list.

title: Print an lss_audit object — print.lss_audit
text: audit object, invisibly.

title: Read a LimeSurvey .lss file — read_lss
text: Read LimeSurvey survey structure export (.lss, XML file) turn structured lss object rest package can audit (audit_lss()) render (render_questionnaire(), render_audit()). Parsing fully local: file never uploaded anywhere.

title: Read a LimeSurvey .lss file — read_lss
code: read_lss(file)

title: Read a LimeSurvey .lss file — read_lss
text: file Character. Path .lss file. Must single string pointing existing file, otherwise classed error raised (lssdoc_bad_path, lssdoc_file_not_found).

title: Read a LimeSurvey .lss file — read_lss
text: object class lss: list survey languages, metadata, one data frame per .lss section. Structural sections (surveys, groups, questions, subquestions, answers, question_attributes, conditions) stay separate localized text sections (survey_language_settings, group_l10ns, question_l10ns, answer_l10ns), carry per-language titles, labels, help texts. values read verbatim character.

title: Read a LimeSurvey .lss file — read_lss
text: .lss format LimeSurvey XML export. Since DBVersion 4xx/7xx translatable text lives dedicated localization sections (*_l10ns), keyed language, structural sections hold identifiers settings. read_lss() reads every section tidy data frame without mutating user-facing identifier text. field present empty (e.g. <help/>) read ""; field absent row read NA.

title: Read a LimeSurvey .lss file — read_lss
code: # A synthetic four-language demo survey ships with the package. demo <- system.file("extdata", "demo_survey.lss", package = "lssdoc") lss <- read_lss(demo) lss$languages #> [1] "en" "de" "es" "fr"

what: Render the audit as a focused Word or PDF document — render_audit
title: Render the audit as a focused Word or PDF document — render_audit
text: Build short, action-oriented document containing audit findings: cover page full questionnaire document, summary counts, one table per severity (errors, warnings, notes) listing every finding location message. Use QA follow-share issues colleague without distributing full questionnaire.

title: Render the audit as a focused Word or PDF document — render_audit
code: render_audit(   input,   output,   languages = NULL,   logo = NULL,   logo_width = 1.5,   logo_height = 0.75,   font = NULL,   font_code = NULL,   colors = NULL,   authors = NULL,   description = NULL,   chrome_lang = NULL )

title: Render the audit as a focused Word or PDF document — render_audit
text: input Either path .lss file (character string) pre-parsed lss object returned read_lss(). Passing path parses fly; passing lss object avoids re-parsing workflow already inspected audit. output Character. Path file create. extension determines output format: .docx writes Word document directly, .pdf writes Word document temporary location converts locally via LibreOffice (Word, Windows). extension rejected lssdoc_bad_output_ext. languages Character vector language codes used cover page. NULL (default) keeps languages survey declared order. logo Optional path (character) PNG JPEG image displayed top cover page. NULL (default) keeps cover logo-free. logo_width, logo_height Image dimensions inches. Defaults 1.5 0.75, tuned 2:1 logo. Resize pre-crop image fit different aspect ratio. font Optional body font name (character). NULL (default) keeps Calibri. See render_questionnaire() guidance overrides. font_code Optional monospace font (character) used code-like content (variable codes, raw expressions). NULL (default) keeps Consolas. colors Optional named list hex color overrides editorial petrol-blue palette. NULL (default) keeps package palette intact. shape accepted names render_questionnaire(). authors, description Optional cover-page credit block (authors) free-form note (description). NULL (default) . shapes render_questionnaire(). chrome_lang Language used document chrome (column headers, row labels, audit section). One "en", "fr", "de", "es", "". NULL (default) follows languages[1] supported, otherwise falls back "en".

title: Render the audit as a focused Word or PDF document — render_audit
text: output path, invisibly.

title: Render the audit as a focused Word or PDF document — render_audit
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # One-shot (path -> .docx) render_audit(   system.file("extdata", "demo_survey.lss",               package = "lssdoc"),   tempfile(fileext = ".docx") )  # PDF output -- same call, just pass a .pdf path render_audit("survey.lss", "qa.pdf") } # }

what: Render a LimeSurvey questionnaire to a Word or PDF document — render_questionnaire
title: Render a LimeSurvey questionnaire to a Word or PDF document — render_questionnaire
text: Build professional questionnaire document LimeSurvey survey, displaying four languages side side. question becomes compact flextable meta header (variable code, type, mandatory, filter) shown , language column headers, question text per language, subquestion answer-option rows underneath – codes left, labels per language right. Headings, metadata cover page, optional table contents, optional audit summary tie document together. Rendering uses suggested packages officer flextable; must installed.

title: Render a LimeSurvey questionnaire to a Word or PDF document — render_questionnaire
code: render_questionnaire(   input,   output,   languages = NULL,   template = c("cards", "table"),   layout = c("auto", "side-by-side", "stacked"),   show_audit = TRUE,   show_help = TRUE,   show_attrs = c("prefix", "suffix", "other_replace_text", "validation"),   show_technical_attrs = FALSE,   page_format = c("auto", "A4-portrait", "A4-landscape", "A3"),   show_toc = TRUE,   show_index = TRUE,   show_quotas = TRUE,   show_header_title = TRUE,   show_source = TRUE,   show_item_heading = FALSE,   show_raw_filter = FALSE,   show_groups = TRUE,   show_welcome = TRUE,   show_endtext = TRUE,   show_description = TRUE,   show_consent = TRUE,   show_privacy_settings = FALSE,   show_admin_settings = FALSE,   title = NULL,   logo = NULL,   logo_width = 1.5,   logo_height = 0.75,   font = NULL,   font_code = NULL,   colors = NULL,   authors = NULL,   description = NULL,   chrome_lang = NULL,   variable_names = c("brackets", "underscore"),   base_size = 10L )

title: Render a LimeSurvey questionnaire to a Word or PDF document — render_questionnaire
text: input Either path .lss file (character string) pre-parsed lss object returned read_lss(). Passing path parses fly. Passing lss object avoids re-parsing survey rendered different options (e.g. multiple language subsets) session. output Character. Path file create. extension determines output format: .docx writes Word document directly, .pdf writes Word document temporary location converts locally via LibreOffice (Word, Windows). extension rejected lssdoc_bad_output_ext. languages Character vector language codes display, order appear columns. NULL (default) keeps languages found .lss file order <languages> section. Acts subset filter ordering: e.g. c("en", "fr") puts English first, c("fr", "en") puts French first. languages[1] treated primary language (TOC entries, group fallback). Requesting language absent survey error (lssdoc_unknown_language). template Output style. One "cards" (default) "table". "cards" renders one detached pair tables per item (meta table + item table), stacked vertically, content languages displayed side--side item table. "table" renders single dense table covering whole document: every variable one tinted Question row carrying | Variable | Type | Mand. | Filter, followed one white Value rows. Group banners (group's description, author wrote one) become section rows; column header repeats every page. body font steps three four languages columns stay within portrait content width. layout Reserved future use. Currently "auto" . show_audit Logical. TRUE (default), include audit summary section near top inline markers questions carry findings. Set FALSE clean reading copy. show_help Logical. TRUE (default), include question help texts question text. show_attrs Character vector question attributes surface question text present. Default keeps attributes change respondents see item: "prefix", "suffix", "other_replace_text", "validation". Add "exclude_all_others" "exclude_all_others_auto" also surface row-level exclusivity flags (debug-style). Pass character(0) hide . show_technical_attrs Logical. TRUE, include technical attributes answer_order location_*. FALSE (default) hides . page_format Page format. One "auto" (default), "A4-portrait", "A4-landscape", "A3". "auto" template-aware never follows language count (four languages fit A4 portrait): "cards" template gets A4 portrait, dense "table" template gets A4 landscape, needs stay readable. Pass explicit value override; selecting wider page makes every panel (meta table, item table, audit/quota tables dense table) expand automatically fill content width, templates. show_toc Logical. TRUE (default), include table contents listing groups (skipped automatically survey fewer two groups). per-variable navigation, use show_index. show_index Logical. TRUE (default), append variable index end document listing every item code number, sorted alphabetically. show_quotas Logical. TRUE (default), append quotas section (end text, variable index) listing sampling quota: localized name, status (active, limit action full), membership condition resolved question codes answer labels, localized "quota full" message. Skipped survey defines quotas. show_header_title Logical. TRUE (default), show survey title top right every page (one line per displayed language, truncated 80 characters). FALSE keeps X/Y page counter bottom right. show_source Logical. TRUE (default), show Source file name Survey ID rows cover metadata table. Pass FALSE hide (e.g. sharing without exposing LimeSurvey internals). show_item_heading Logical. FALSE (default), meta table starts item directly, compact layout. TRUE, bold "N. variable" heading added item scroll-time navigation. show_raw_filter Logical. FALSE (default), Filter cell shows human-readable form (e.g. Q1 = 1) – editorial style, matching ESS / MOSAiCH / GESIS conventions. Set TRUE also surface raw LimeSurvey relevance expression underneath small italic gray (e.g. !is_empty(Q1.NAOK) && (Q1.NAOK == 1)), useful QA cross-checks. raw form always shown plain form simplified. show_groups Logical. TRUE (default), show group banners (cards layout) group rows (table layout). Pass FALSE flatten document single sequence items section breaks (useful groups exist internal organization). show_welcome Logical. TRUE (default), include survey's multilingual welcome text (surveyls_welcometext) side--side block (cards) embedded row (table). show_endtext Logical. TRUE (default), include survey's multilingual end text (surveyls_endtext). treatment show_welcome. show_description Logical. TRUE (default), include survey's multilingual description (surveyls_description) – "survey " intro LimeSurvey shows welcome text landing page. show_consent Logical. TRUE (default), render data protection consent block front matter (welcome text): survey's privacy policy notice (surveyls_policy_notice) consent checkbox label (surveyls_policy_notice_label), side side across languages, checkbox drawn empty box. Skipped survey turns policy notice carries notice text. show_privacy_settings Logical. FALSE (default), omit survey-level privacy / tracking flags cover. Set TRUE surface anonymized, save partial, datestamp, ipaddr, refurl rows – useful ethics committee submissions. show_admin_settings Logical. FALSE (default), omit survey-level administrative settings. Set TRUE surface alias, end URL description, active flag rows. title Optional override survey title shown cover top-right header. NULL (default) uses per-language titles .lss survey settings. Pass single string use title every displayed language, named character vector keyed language code (e.g. c(fr = "Mon titre", de = "Mein Titel")) per-language overrides. logo Optional path (character) PNG JPEG image displayed top cover page. NULL (default) keeps cover logo-free, matching neutral style survey-methodology references (ESS, MOSAiCH, Panel). .lss file embed logo, image must supplied caller. logo_width, logo_height Image dimensions inches. Defaults 1.5 0.75, tuned 2:1 logo. Resize pre-crop image fit different aspect ratio. font Optional body font name (character). NULL (default) keeps Calibri, pre-installed every recent Windows Office metric-substituted Carlito (OFL) Mac Linux LibreOffice, column widths stay stable across platforms. Pass string override (e.g. "Source Sans 3", "IBM Plex Sans", corporate brand font); install font machine opens document, otherwise reader's application substitutes fallback face. font_code Optional monospace font name (character) used code-like content: variable column meta table, raw relevance expression filter cell, variable index entries. NULL (default) keeps Consolas; pass "JetBrains Mono" "IBM Plex Mono" sharper code style. colors Optional named list hex color overrides editorial petrol-blue palette. NULL (default) keeps package palette intact. Accepted names: "primary" (group filets, headers text item top borders), "accent" (hyperlinks, ORCID iD, URL auto-links, group -line cards), "band" (light header backgrounds), "band_dark" (meta-table dark header cards), "zebra" (-light tint Question rows table template), "grid" (0.5 pt border color), "text", "muted". value must hex string ("#XXXXXX" "#XXX"). Unknown keys rejected (lssdoc_bad_colors). Useful honoring institutional brand: e.g. colors = list(primary = "#5C9F1A", accent = "#7FA82E") produces LimeSurvey-green document. authors Optional credit block questionnaire's designers, displayed cover page subtitle. author shown centered line Name -- Affiliation; ORCID iD provided, smaller monospace line shows ORCID 0000-0000-0000-0000 hyperlink https://orcid.org/<id>. Accepts: NULL (default): authorship block. unnamed character vector (c("Jane Doe", "John Doe")): entry becomes line affiliation. named character vector (c("Jane Doe" = "HESAV")): names authors, values affiliations. Use "" render author without affiliation. list named lists full form, e.g. list(list(name = "Jane Doe", affiliation = "HESAV", orcid = "0009-0001-2345-6789"), list(name = "John Doe", affiliation = "HESAV")). name field required; affiliation orcid optional. description Optional free-form text (single string) shown cover page authors block. NULL (default) omits block. Useful citation hint, funding acknowledgement, methodology note, link related publication. Line breaks (\n) split block separate centered lines; http:// https:// tokens rendered clickable hyperlinks. chrome_lang Language used chrome document (column headers, row labels, navigation titles, type labels, Value descriptors, audit section). One "en", "fr", "de", "es", "". NULL (default) follows languages[1] supported, otherwise falls back "en". Independent languages, controls survey's content columns: e.g. chrome_lang = "en" languages = c("fr", "en") produces English-labelled document French English content. Spanish Italian translations reviewed native speaker publishing official document. variable_names response-variable names written, document matches data file reader holds. One : "brackets" (default) – exact column names CSV / Excel data export, variable index reproduces raw data file column column: parent[subq], parent[subq][1] (dual scale), parent[CH] / parent[] (multiple choice), parent[59842] (ranking, answer id), parent[_Ccomment] (list--comment). "underscore" – sanitized code form used Expression Manager / relevance equations SPSS / Stata / R exports (parent_subq, parent_subq_1). two-dimensional arrays (array numbers / texts second axis) ranking questions expanded every produced column appears entry either way. base_size Body type size points (default 10). One lever scales whole document: question text, item tables, meta band, quotas table, variable index cover metadata follow , headings answer/help text keep relative offsets. Useful roomier single-language render (e.g. 12). cover title subtitle keep fixed title-page sizes. Accepted range: 7 16.

title: Render a LimeSurvey questionnaire to a Word or PDF document — render_questionnaire
text: output path, invisibly.

what: "LimeSurvey last save" date on the cover
title: Render a LimeSurvey questionnaire to a Word or PDF document — render_questionnaire
text: cover metadata table carries row labelled "LimeSurvey last save" (localized equivalent). read verbatim surveys.lastmodified column .lss, timestamp LimeSurvey writes export – table (questions, question_l10ns, answer_l10ns, groups, etc.) carries per-row modification date. row named "last save" rather "last modified" LimeSurvey bumps field reliably user clicks Save survey-level form (Settings tab); editing question text, answer label, translation Question Editor consistently update across LimeSurvey versions. date looks stale relative recent edits, workaround open Survey settings LimeSurvey, click Save (change needed), re-export .lss. next render show bumped timestamp.

title: Render a LimeSurvey questionnaire to a Word or PDF document — render_questionnaire
text: Opening rendered .docx Microsoft Word may surface security-style prompt: "document contains fields may refer files. want update fields document?". expected: package marks page-number bookmark-reference fields needing refresh footer shows correct page count table contents links resolve right pages first open (also makes headless PDF conversion via LibreOffice produce correctly paginated output without manual F9). Clicking Yes safe – document INCLUDETEXT, INCLUDEPICTURE-linked, DDE fields; external links ORCID DOI URLs cover credits, static HYPERLINK targets fetched update.

title: Render a LimeSurvey questionnaire to a Word or PDF document — render_questionnaire
text: output ends .pdf, function first renders .docx temporary location converts locally via LibreOffice headless (Word Windows). LibreOffice (soffice executable) must installed PATH; otherwise classed error explains install . Conversion stays user's machine: upload, network call. LibreOffice headless refresh Word field values (TOC, page counts) conversion, table contents may appear empty converted PDF. obtain PDF populated TOC, render .docx instead, open Word (TOC refreshes automatically) use File > Save > PDF.

title: Render a LimeSurvey questionnaire to a Word or PDF document — render_questionnaire
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ file <- system.file("extdata", "demo_survey.lss", package = "lssdoc")  # One-shot: parse + render Word document render_questionnaire(file, tempfile(fileext = ".docx"))  # Same call, PDF output (format inferred from extension) render_questionnaire(file, tempfile(fileext = ".pdf"))  # Parse once, render several variants without re-parsing lss <- read_lss(file) render_questionnaire(lss, tempfile(fileext = ".docx"),                      languages = "en") render_questionnaire(lss, tempfile(fileext = ".docx"),                      template = "table",                      languages = c("en", "fr"))  # Branded cover with authors block and palette override render_questionnaire(   lss,   tempfile(fileext = ".docx"),   template    = "table",   chrome_lang = "en",   colors      = list(primary = "#5C9F1A", accent = "#7FA82E"),   authors     = list(     list(name = "Jane Doe", affiliation = "HESAV",          orcid = "0009-0001-2345-6789"),     list(name = "John Doe", affiliation = "HESAV",          orcid = "0009-0002-3456-7890")   ) ) } # }
